response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with a genuine developer-tooling background — including hands-on work on a commercial C IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the core stack of this role is missing. There is no substantive Java or Kotlin experience beyond incidental use, no Spring or enterprise JVM framework exposure, and no evidence of deep JVM understanding. Their strengths lie in TypeScript/Node and DLang systems work, which does not transfer directly to enterprise JVM tooling requirements.
strengths: Prior IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong developer-tooling and testing background (built test runner, assertion library, CI infrastructure)
Broad, long-tenured engineering experience across web, embedded, and low-latency systems
concerns: No meaningful Java/Kotlin expertise or deep JVM knowledge, the role's core requirement
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise framework experience
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node full-stack, far from enterprise JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling experience
